note: create-1k: octane must not fall to >2x React's create time (known ~0.8-1.0x; generous headroom).
partial update: octane vs React (known ~1x).
swap rows: LIS keyed reconcile vs React (known <1x).

rotate-backward is the LIS-vs-lastPlacedIndex differentiator: octane moves 1 node, React moves all survivors (known ~12x faster → 0.08x). Guard 0.6 leaves huge headroom while still catching a fast-path break.
Cold production mount of both 1000-row walls (2000 rows total): Octane and React are currently near parity (~0.95x); 1.5 leaves standard noise headroom while catching a structural mount regression.
Production autoMemo skips the unchanged RowsA region before the keyed wall; if dependency-scoped reuse breaks this rises toward the vanilla React memo-wall cost.
full per-cell update churn: octane's per-binding diff vs React (known <1x).
octane server render() vs react-dom renderToString at 50 cards (known ~0.6x → octane faster). Guard 1.2 tolerates noise but catches an octane SSR regression.
intra-octane de-opt cliff: plain-.ts createElement vs compiled .tsrx SSR (known ~2.1x). Bounds the cliff so a broken fast path (ratio→1) or a runaway deopt (ratio>4) both surface.
Rows TOTAL gzip vs ripple, same app, normalized minify. Final compiled-output pass measured 17448 / 12814 = 1.362x on 2026-07-15; ceiling ratcheted 2.05 -> 1.40.

gzip(minified compiled corpus) vs gzip(source corpus). Final 16-file corpus measured 22512 / 22840 = 0.986x on 2026-07-15; compiled output is now smaller than source after gzip.

10-level nested-use() init vs React 19. Compiler-parallelized use (docs/suspense-parallel-use-plan.md, recorded 2026-07-09) collapses the waterfall to the parallel floor: measured 0.065x (20.1ms vs 307.3ms). Guard at 0.25x so a regression back toward per-level rounds fails loudly.
transition-driven refetch vs React 19 (measured 0.10x on record 2026-07-09 - 19.1ms vs 190.1ms, parallel refetch via warm adoption).
init vs Solid 2.0 (parallel by model): measured 1.06x on record 2026-07-09 (20.1ms vs 19.0ms). The compiler's waterfall-elimination win must not regress.
init vs ripple (parallel by model): measured 1.05x on record 2026-07-09 (20.1ms vs 19.2ms).
update vs Solid 2.0: measured 1.03x on record 2026-07-09.
update vs ripple: measured 1.09x on record 2026-07-09.

Cross-framework cold-mount topology guard: Octane reaches the honest 2-wave dependency floor vs React's 6 waves in the 2026-07-17 record. The 1.5x ratio headroom allows reference improvements; the suite separately enforces Octane's exact seven-resource independent first wave and eight-call ceiling.

Cross-framework transition topology guard: Octane reaches the honest 2-wave dependency floor vs React's 3 waves in the 2026-07-17 record. The 1.5x ratio headroom allows reference improvements; the suite separately enforces Octane's exact seven-resource independent first wave and eight-call ceiling.
Deterministic DOM census at 1k rows. Marker reuse leaves Octane at 10074 vs React 10072 nodes (2026-07-13); catches structural-node regressions without timing noise.
Semantic control for the node census: equivalent fixtures must render exactly the same 8051 elements.
Semantic control for the node census: equivalent fixtures must render exactly the same 2021 text nodes.
A stamped sole-component row must share its host boundary: TSRX naive and tuned now both render 10074 nodes instead of the naive fixture carrying 2000 extra comments.
Return-JSX single-root stamping plus keyed boundary sharing removes the JSX naive fixture's 4000 extra comments; its DOM shape must stay equal to tuned.
100-item TodoMVC DOM census: Octane 730 vs React 623 nodes after dormant-if/list-anchor elision (1.172x, 2026-07-13).
Semantic control: equivalent TodoMVC states render exactly 517 elements.
Semantic control: equivalent TodoMVC states render exactly 106 text nodes.
Mixed chat conversation census: Octane 104 vs React 80 nodes after host-vs-host keyed-item boundary sharing (1.30x, 2026-07-13).
Semantic control: equivalent chat states render exactly 55 elements.
Semantic control: equivalent chat states render exactly 25 text nodes.
Closed portal swarm #main census: Octane 2622 vs React 2014 nodes after component-item and dormant-if elision (1.302x, 2026-07-13).
Semantic control: closed portal fixtures render exactly 1411 elements under #main.
Semantic control: closed portal fixtures render exactly 603 text nodes under #main.
Open portal swarm whole-body census retains load-bearing foreign target pairs: Octane 7025 vs React 5217 nodes (1.347x, 2026-07-13).
Semantic control: open portal fixtures render exactly 3412 elements across main and foreign targets.
Semantic control: open portal fixtures render exactly 1805 text nodes across main and foreign targets.
Production-browser mount of 1,000 real Three meshes. The refreshed 10-sample record is 2.64 / 2.52 = 1.05x; a 100-sample stability run recorded 2.612 / 2.655 = 0.98x on 2026-07-17.

In-place position update of 1,000 retained meshes. The refreshed 10-sample record is 0.68 / 0.58 = 1.17x; a 100-sample stability run recorded 0.525 / 0.510 = 1.03x on 2026-07-17, within the sub-millisecond timer noise floor.
Keyed reverse with survivor identity retained. The refreshed observable-commit baseline recorded 3.30 / 2.02 = 1.63x on 2026-07-17.
Removal of the rendered 1,000-mesh tree, excluding delayed root-registry teardown. The refreshed observable-commit baseline recorded 1.84 / 2.12 = 0.87x on 2026-07-17.
Constructor reconstruction plus observed disposal for 1,000 objects. The refreshed observable-commit baseline recorded 9.28 / 2.00 = 4.64x on 2026-07-17.
Per-frame cost for 1,000 useFrame subscribers, averaged over 20 manual frames. The refreshed observable-commit baseline recorded 0.059 / 0.032 = 1.84x on 2026-07-17.
Native pointer dispatch and deduplicated Three raycast delivery to 40 overlapping targets. The refreshed observable-commit baseline recorded 0.348 / 0.327 = 1.06x on 2026-07-17.
Minimal direct-root production gzip, with built bundles executed and scene-checked in Chromium. The refreshed observable-scene baseline recorded 305165 / 188008 = 1.62x on 2026-07-17.
Full-catalogue production gzip, with namespace retention and a real Mesh checked in Chromium. The refreshed observable-scene baseline recorded 304757 / 250306 = 1.22x on 2026-07-17.

Per-island delegated-listener cost must stay flat as island count scales (currently exactly 1.0x at 5 listeners/island). Growth here means a super-linear structural regression in delegation-target registration; Phase 5's selective hosted delegation may only LOWER the empty-island cost.
Exactly one owner-bridge binding per hosted root regardless of island count (currently 1.0x). Growth means duplicated bindings or a leaked rebind per render.
